channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: US colleges returning to campus sexual assault rules created during Trump's first term
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Schools and universities responding to complaints of sexual misconduct must return to policies created during President Donald Trump's first term, with requirements for live hearings and more protections for accused students, according to new guidance issued Friday by the Education
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Officials search river where a passenger jet and Army chopper crashed, and probe the cause
item.description: American Airlines Flight 5342 with 60 passengers and four crew hit an Army helicopter near Washington D.C.'s Reagan National Wednesday night, sending the two aircraft into the Potomac River and killing all aboard in the deadliest U.S. air crash in more than two decades. The crash around 9 p.m.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels move deeper into eastern Congo as UN reports executions and rapes
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Rwanda-backed rebels were expanding their presence in eastern Congo after capturing Goma, the region's major city, the U.N.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU and JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Robert Santos decides to resign as US Census Bureau director midway through a 5-year term
item.description: Robert Santos, who emphasized inclusivity and outreach to overlooked communities, has decided to resign as director of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Flight recorders recovered in DCA crash
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) said Thursday that they recovered flight data recorders, known as black boxes, from the aircraft involved in the deadly mid-air collision near Reagan Washington National Airport earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: Trump makes his first moves on education, but no McMahon hearing in sight
item.description: President Trump made his first major moves on education Wednesday, signing a series of executive orders that come even before his nominee to lead the Education Department, Linda McMahon, has had her Senate confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Congress authorized more flights at Reagan National despite warnings
item.description: Congress added more flights to Reagan National's schedule last year despite warnings from the airport.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump warns Canada, Mexico tariffs are coming on Saturday
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday said he plans to follow through on Saturday on his threat to impose 25 percent tariffs on goods from Mexico and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Marianne Faithfull, singer and pop icon, dies at 78
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Marianne Faithfull, the British pop star, muse, libertine and old soul who inspired and helped write some of the Rolling Stones' greatest songs and endured as a torch singer and survivor of the lifestyle she once embodied, has died. She was 78.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Divers return to Potomac River for DC plane crash recovery and investigation
item.description: Air crash investigations can take months, and federal investigators told reporters they would not speculate on the cause.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: What to know about the NTSB and the investigation into the DC plane crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A collision between an American Airlines passenger jet and an Army helicopter near Washington, D.C., that killed 67 people brought renewed focus on the federal agency charged with investigating aviation disasters.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Carolina prepares for third execution since September
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — South Carolina is preparing to execute the third inmate to be put to death since September as the state goes through a backlog of prisoners who exhausted their appeals while the state couldn't find lethal injection drugs. Marion Bowman Jr.'s execution is scheduled for 6 p.m.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Student, attorney among Ohioans killed in D.C. plane crash
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) -- A 20-year-old student at an Ohio university was among those killed Wednesday night when an American Airlines passenger jet collided with an Army helicopter in Washington D.C. Grace Maxwell was a junior at Cedarville University.
item.dc:creator: Brian Hofmann, NewsNation

item.title: Carmel, Indiana, woman among those killed in DC passenger jet, helicopter crash
item.description: (WXIN/WTTV): An Indiana University Alumna was among the 67 people killed when a plane and a military helicopter collided near the Ronald Reagan National Airport in Washington, D.C., on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Haughn, NewsNation

item.title: Honolulu police have arrested 6 people in connection with deadly New Year’s fireworks blast
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Police arrested a total of six people in connection with a deadly explosion of illegal fireworks in a Honolulu neighborhood on New Year’s Eve and confiscated 500 pounds (227 kilograms) of unused fireworks from the scene of the blast, authorities said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: MMA fighter praises Hitler, calls him a 'good guy' in podcast
item.description: UFC fighter Bryce Mitchell praised Adolf Hitler and denied the Holocaust happened in the first episode of his new podcast.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KRON4

item.title: Neo-Nazis charged with grooming, extorting California children to make pornographic, violent material
item.description: Four members of a neo-Nazi online group have been charged with federal crimes accusing them of grooming and extorting more than a dozen children to produce pornographic media and violent videos of self-harm.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Aviation expert weighs in on how aircraft crash near DC may have happened
item.description: An aviation expert told Nexstar's WDCW that the altitude of both the passenger plane and Army helicopter may have played a principal role in the aircraft collision near Reagan National Airport (DCA).
item.dc:creator: Anna Chen, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Marianne Faithfull, singer, actress and Rolling Stones muse, dies at 78
item.description: The 1960s icon passed in London surrounded by family, according to a statement from her record promotion group.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DC crash ‘almost identical’ to 1949 collision: Aviation expert
item.description: “There was a prior accident at Washington National 75 years ago almost identical to what happened last night.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, KRON4

item.title: FCC chair launches investigation into NPR, PBS
item.description: The chairman of the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) is launching an investigation into NPR and PBS over their alleged "airing of commercials.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KRON4

item.title: NASA's 2 stuck astronauts take their first spacewalk together
item.description: NASA’s two stuck astronauts took their first spacewalk together Thursday, exiting the International Space Station almost eight months after moving in.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What we know about the DC plane crash victims
item.description: Among those killed were figure skaters, union members, and a couple en route to visit their child at college.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, KRON4

item.title: Trump holds briefing on plane crash near DC, suggests DEI programs contributed
item.description: President Donald Trump held a briefing Thursday morning on the deadly collision between an American Airlines plane and a military helicopter over the Potomac River.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: No survivors after collision between American Airlines jet, Army helicopter near DC
item.description: Everyone aboard an American Airlines jet carrying 60 passengers and four crew members that collided with an Army helicopter was feared dead in what was likely to be the worst U.S. aviation disaster in almost a quarter century, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Chen, KRON4

item.title: Figure skaters among those on flight that collided with Army helicopter
item.description: U.S. Figure Skating released a statement saying athletes, coaches, and family members were on their way home from the National Development Camp held in conjunction with the U.S. Figure Skating Championship in Kansas when the collision happened.
item.dc:creator: Brian Farrell, KRON4

item.title: Passenger jet with 64 aboard collides with Army helicopter while landing at Reagan Airport near DC
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — An American Airlines jet carrying 60 passengers and four crew members collided Wednesday with an Army helicopter while landing at Ronald Reagan National Airport near Washington, prompting a large search-and-rescue operation in the nearby Potomac River.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, TARA COPP and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Are we all aliens? NASA's returned asteroid samples hold the ingredients of life from a watery world
item.description: Asteroid samples fetched by NASA hold not only the pristine building blocks for life but also the salty remains of an ancient water world..
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Video captures fiery crash between American Airlines flight and Army Blackhawk
item.description: Editor's note: The video may appear graphic to some viewers. (NEXSTAR) - Images from the EarthCam video service captured images of what appears to be the fiery crash of an American Airlines jet over the Potomac River Wednesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5

item.title: Regional jet carrying 64, military helicopter on training flight collide near Reagan National Airport
item.description: A regional jet carrying 60 passengers and four crew members collided with a military helicopter on Wednesday night as it approached Washington Reagan National Airport. The flight was en route from Wichita, Kan., to Washington, D.C., American Airlines said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, WGN 9

item.title: Trump White House rescinds memo freezing federal money after widespread confusion
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's budget office on Wednesday rescinded a memo freezing spending on federal loans and grants, less than two days after it sparked widespread confusion and legal challenges across the country.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, ZEKE MILLER and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Democrats demand Patel information in special counsel's classified documents report
item.description: Democrats on the Senate Judiciary Committee are demanding to see the volume of former special counsel Jack Smith’s report dealing with the Mar-a-Lago classified documents investigation, asserting it has relevant information about Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to lead the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5
